channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Lawsuit Claims OpenAI Chatbot Gave Advice That Led to Fatal Overdose
item.description: The lawsuit seeks monetary damages and asks the court to pause OpenAI’s rollout of ChatGPT Health, a platform the company announced in January that allows users to upload medical records and receive personalized health advice. The parents of a man who died of an accidental drug overdose last year sued OpenAI and its founder and CEO Sam Altman in a California court on Tuesday, alleging the man was coached to take a dangerous combination of substances by ChatGPT. Related: Family of FSU Mass Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI The combination of those drugs and alcohol resulted in Nelson’s death in May 2025, according to the lawsuit, which was filed in state court in San Francisco.

item.title: India launches US$1.5bn maritime insurance pool, issues first war risk policies
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Axa XL launches prevention unit, led by global CUO Libby Benet
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA reviews whether investment firms are doing enough to support bereaved customers
item.description: The FCA is reviewing how consumer investment firms support bereaved customers and whether they're getting it right. Fewer than half of bereaved customers (47%) felt they received the support they needed from financial firms, according to research (PDF) . It will assess how firms communicate, how they support vulnerable customers, their service standards, and how fees are handled on bereaved accounts.

item.title: FCA announces new appointments to executive team
item.description: The FCA has announced 2 permanent appointments to its executive team, strengthening leadership at a pivotal time for UK and global financial markets. As the leading and largest European regulator, the FCA leads from the front in many regulated areas by protecting customers, driving smarter regulation, and combatting financial crime through collaboration and the use of AI.' Nikhil Rathi, chief executive of the FCA said: 'A strong leadership team is a crucial part of us being the smarter regulator we aspire to be. Simon Walls has been appointed permanent executive director, markets.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Agtech Startups Face A Drier Funding Climate
item.description: The broad trend: Venture funding in agriculture and farming is still undergoing a market correction following the hyper-funding era of 2021, when startups in the space raised $10.5 billion across 1,419 deals, a review of Crunchbase data shows. Many agtech startups that raised massive rounds during that 2021 peak have struggled to show real-world traction. The numbers: So far this year through May 7, agtech startups have raised $1.4 billion, on track to be down slightly or on par with the $4.4 billion raised last year and the $4.6 billion raised in 2024.

item.title: Exclusive: Physician-Founded Saile Raises $2.2M To Help Doctors Find Side Jobs Using AI
item.description: Marc Ayoub , a neurosurgeon born into a family of doctors, the path to entrepreneurship was a necessity born of frustration. “The bottleneck is not the number of doctors, but the fragmented infrastructure connecting them to where they are needed,” said Ayoub, who also serves as an assistant professor of neurosurgery at the Donald & Barbara Zucker School of Medicine. “Most people assume the issue in healthcare staffing is a lack of doctors, but what we’ve seen is something different.

item.title: Northern Re expands use of Ultrassure platform to support reinsurance contract operations
item.description: Northern Re, the collateralized reinsurance company that provides investors with access to the returns of its long-tail casualty underwriting business, has expanded its use of Ultrassure’s platform from an initial pilot to production deployment for contract workflows across its reinsurance operations. During the pilot, Northern Re and Ultrassure reportedly worked together to refine the platform’s fit for day-to-day contract work, with a particular focus on usability, responsiveness to feedback, and operational readiness. The production rollout also reflects Northern Re’s choice to transition the platform into active operation following its evaluation in live workflows and the analysis of its potential to enhance contract operations with improved consistency, efficiency, and control.

item.title: Mangrove now aims for up to $111m of named storm reinsurance from Buttonwood Re cat bond
item.description: Now, we’re told that this debut cat bond under Buttonwood Re now targets between the initial $100 million and up to $111 million of named storm reinsurance protection in the state of Florida for Mangrove. Mangrove Property Insurance is now aiming to secure up to $111 million of named storm reinsurance protection from its first Buttonwood Re Ltd. For its first catastrophe bond sponsorship under Buttonwood Re, Mangrove is aiming to secure both occurrence and aggregate reinsurance to protect its business against losses from named storms and hurricanes in the state of Florida.

item.title: Compre sees repeatable opportunity in George Street Re sidecar: CUO Bardon
item.description: Rachel Bardon, Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO) at Compre, said its recent participation in George Street Re, QBE Re’s debut casualty sidecar , represents a repeatable and well-diversified book of business, exemplifying the company’s broader strategy as it looks to pursue similar transactions in the future. In an interview with Artemis during our recent visit to Bermuda, Bardon explained that the George Street Re sidecar marks a strong first transaction for Compre in the space, noting that that the firm’s participation benefits the cedent by expanding the pool of potential investors. It’s a defined block that we like.” Bardon explained that the arrangement sees Compre take a slice of the sidecar from day one for the prospective underwriting year, while also providing an exit solution for the other investors in it.

item.title: Alternative capital redesigning reinsurance, but still has challenges to face: Guy Carpenter
item.description: Alternative capital is rewriting the rules of reinsurance, and while it seems it is here to stay, its rapid integration brings distinct structural challenges, a recent Guy Carpenter report highlights. The ‘Convergence of Financial and Reinsurance Capital: How catastrophe bonds, sidecars, and financial investors are redesigning the architecture of global reinsurance’ report examines the accelerating integration of alternative capital into the global reinsurance market and the implications for capacity, pricing, and market structure. According to Guy Carpenter’s data, alternative capital now represents nearly 20% of the estimated US$660 billion in global reinsurance capital, up from 13% in 2013.

item.title: Talanx sees €774m net income and 88.7% CoR in Q1’26
item.description: Talanx has announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2026, reporting a 28% year on year rise in net income, to EUR 774 million, and an improved combined ratio of 88.7%. Talanx reported net income growth in all divisions and a positive claims experience following unusually high loss payments in the prior-year quarter. These figures compare with EUR 604 million in net income and a 92.8% combined ratio seen in Q1 2025.

item.title: Aon strengthens data and analytics capabilities with Claims Copilot expansion
item.description: “The platform is a core component of Aon’s broader investment in data and analytics capabilities, alongside Aon Broker Copilot and the firm’s Risk Analyzers and Diagnostics tools.” Aon noted that it will continue to expand Claims Copilot across additional EMEA and Latin American markets over the coming months, further strengthening its role as a foundational platform within the firm’s global Commercial Risk business. The firm continued, “Aon Claims Copilot brings together claims data and advanced analytics into a single, globally connected platform, enabling more consistent, transparent and data-led claims management for clients around the world. Building on the successful pilot launched in November 2025 , the expansion of Aon Claims Copilot reportedly marks a significant milestone in the platform’s global deployment and adoption.

item.title: Alabama AG Announces Civil Probe of Southern Poverty Law Center
item.description: Alabama’s attorney general announced a civil investigation Monday into the Southern Poverty Law Center’s fundraising practices in the wake of a federal indictment against the organization. Top photo: The Southern Poverty Law Center in Montgomery. Alabama Attorney General Steve Marshall said he has sent a subpoena to the center seeking information about its donations and payments to informants.

item.title: Tesla Authorized to Test Supervised Self-Driving Software in Belgium
item.description: Tesla has been authorized to test its supervised self-driving software on one car on the roads of Belgian region of Flanders as part of a the general authorization process, a spokesperson for regional minister Annick De Ridder said on Wednesday. (Reporting by Inti Landauro, editing by Louise Heavens)

item.title: California Suit Claims OpenAI Chatbot Gave Advice That Led to Fatal Overdose
item.description: The parents of a man who died of an accidental drug overdose last year sued OpenAI and its founder and CEO Sam Altman in a California court on Tuesday, alleging the man was coached to take a dangerous combination of substances by ChatGPT. The lawsuit seeks monetary damages and asks the court to pause OpenAI’s rollout of ChatGPT Health, a platform the company announced in January that allows users to upload medical records and receive personalized health advice. Leila Turner-Scott and Angus Scott say their son, Sam Nelson, 19, was using a chatbot for guidance on combining different drugs.

item.title: Outmarket AI raises $17 million to expand insurance workflow automation platform
item.description: Outmarket AI has secured $17 million in Series A funding as the company looks to accelerate development of its AI-powered insurance workflow platform for agencies and brokerages. Its platform integrates with agency management systems and supports functions such as automated policy reviews, loss run analysis, and workflow automation designed to reduce manual processing across brokerage operations. By combining workflow automation, AI-driven analysis, and data integration, Outmarket aims to help brokerages improve productivity, reduce administrative burdens, and speed up client servicing.

item.title: TWFG acquires Fortress Insurance Services
item.description: TWFG Insurance announced the acquisition of Fortress Insurance Services , adding four agency locations and expanding its presence in the Upper Midwest. “We are very pleased to welcome Fortress Insurance Services into the TWFG family. Fortress Insurance Services offers personal and commercial insurance and is known for its community-focused approach.

item.title: New York Life Ventures backs voice AI startup Vapi
item.description: New York Life Ventures announced an investment in Vapi , a San Francisco-based startup developing infrastructure for AI-powered voice agents, as part of the company’s $50 million Series B round led by Peak XV. According to New York Life Ventures, Vapi has handled more than one billion calls and has over one million developers on its platform. New York Life Ventures highlighted Amazon Ring as a customer, stating the company transitioned all inbound calls to Vapi within two weeks after evaluating multiple vendors.

item.title: Outmarket AI raises $17 million
item.description: Outmarket AI has raised $17 million in Series A funding led by Permanent Capital Ventures, with participation from SignalFire, Fika Ventures, TTV Capital, and Dash Fund. The round also includes strategic investments from “leading independent agency networks, prominent agency owners, and senior industry executives.” This investment brings Outmarket’s total funding to $21.7 million. Founded in 2023, Outmarket delivers intelligent workflows across commercial, benefits, personal lines, and specialty insurance.

item.title: Sabseg sigue su proceso de desarrollo y ahora compra la correduría madrileña PQI
item.description: En este proceso de fortalecimiento de su estructura, este boletín puede anticipar la compra que va a dar a conocer hoy: la correduría madrileña PQI (Premium Quality Investment) , que cuenta con una cartera de más de 5.600 clientes , en su mayoría pymes y empresas del segmento middle market, y que gestiona más de 13 millones de euros en primas intermediadas. La operación, pendiente de la autorización regulatoria, va a ser anunciada por el director general de negocio y red Sabseg, Felipe Cobián, en la imagen, con el director de PQI, Luis Quintana , que destaca la aportación de la nueva correduría en un mercado estratégico para el grupo, como es Madrid. Como venimos anticipando, Sabseg no va a parar hasta cumplir -posiblemente antes de tiempo- su ambicioso plan de crecimiento hasta 2030, que contempla superar los 2.000 millones de euros en primas intermediadas.

item.title: Helix Marine comienza a operar en el negocio de Puertos y Terminales
item.description: Helix Marine ha sido autorizada por la DGSFP para operar en una nueva línea de negocio para Puertos y Terminales. La firma, que está de celebración por su quinto aniversario, detalla que con este producto se va a dar cobertura a operadores portuarios, estibadores, terminales y centros portuarios de empleo, con un amplio abanico de coberturas , desde Responsabilidad Civil, Avería de Maquinaría o Daños Materiales hasta pólizas por Pérdida de Beneficios. «Abordamos esta nueva línea de negocio con mucha ilusión, con el objetivo de aportar al sector portuario español soluciones aseguradoras adecuadas y especializadas «, valora el CEO, Rafael Real.

item.title: Grupo hIB refuerza su visión de futuro y la colaboración entre los socios
item.description: Los socios han tenido la oportunidad de reflexionar y profundizar en cuestiones clave para el futuro de la asociación . Más allá de las sesiones de trabajo, los asistentes destacan la calidad de las conversaciones, la implicación de todos los participantes y el fortalecimiento de los vínculos entre los socios . Grupo hIB ha celebrado por primera vez una Convención de dos días, celebrada en Los Pedroches (Córdoba) el 7 y 8 de mayo.

item.title: Europe’s EV Investments Near $235B, New Automotive Data Shows
item.description: “Europe now produces batteries for roughly one in three EVs sold domestically, and announced capacity could meet future demand if fully utilized,” New Automotive said. Germany accounted for almost a quarter of the region’s investment, making it the largest national hub in Europe’s EV sector, according to New Automotive. Campaign group E-Mobility Europe said the investments already support more than 150,000 jobs, with another 300,000 possible if all announced projects go ahead.

item.title: PayPal Agrees to Settle US Probe of DEI Business Initiative
item.description: A PayPal spokesperson said the company would be launching the small business initiative “to infuse American small businesses with even more economic opportunity.” The Wall Street Journal reported earlier on the settlement. Justice Department probe into allegations the company had violated federal civil rights laws when it started an initiative to support minority-owned businesses. The payment platform also plans to start a small business initiative, which would include fee waivers for veterans and businesses engaged in farming manufacturing or technology, according to the settlement agreement.

item.title: AAA Forecasts Record Memorial Day Drivers Despite High Gas Prices
item.description: A record number of people are expected to travel by car over Memorial Day weekend, a sign of resilient demand for fuel amid surging pump prices, at least for now. Average gasoline prices are at their highest seasonal level on record, and sit around 50 cents off all-time highs set in June 2022, according to AAA data. That doesn’t mean high prices won’t begin precluding consumers from buying gas down the line, with $5 a gallon gas a legitimate possibility, JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Swiss Re confirms Yusuke Matsumoto as head of APAC facultative reinsurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Ardonagh continues to strengthen Asia leadership with Jason Anthony appointment
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Howden names Alaric Lee as Hong Kong CEO, replaces Singapore-bound Alfred Sham
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Florida Citizens secures one-third upsized $600m Everglades Re II 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: Now, we’ve learned that Florida Citizens has been successful and secured the one-third upsized $600 million of reinsurance limit from this Everglades Re II 2026-1 cat bond deal, while the notes have now been priced at below guidance levels. Florida Citizens returned to the catastrophe bond market in April , with an initial target to secure $450 million of named storm reinsurance protection from this new Everglades Re II 2026-1 cat bond deal. Florida’s Citizens Property Insurance Corporation has now secured its one-third upsized target for reinsurance protection from its new Everglades Re II Ltd.

item.title: Aon expands global cyber team with senior industry hires
item.description: Aon, the professional services and risk advisory company, has appointed Jason Warmbir, Petra Stojilova and Matt Danielak to its global cyber team as the business looks to further strengthen its cyber risk advisory capabilities. Warmbir, Stojilova and Danielak bring extensive industry experience and will report to Brent Rieth, Aon’s global cyber leader. Aon said the new hires will work across the company’s cyber capabilities within its Commercial Risk division, helping clients improve how they assess, quantify and manage cyber exposure.

item.title: Allianz sees record operating profit of €4.5bn for Q1’26
item.description: Global insurer Allianz has reported record operating profit of €4.5 billion for the first quarter of 2026, a rise of 6.6% compared to €4.2 billion in Q1’25, as total business volume for the quarter dipped slightly to €53 billion from €54 billion. The segment reported a record operating profit of €2.4 billion in Q1’26, compared to €2.2 billion in the comparative prior year quarter, reaching 27% of the firm’s full-year outlook midpoint. There was a 11.1% growth in operating profit, entirely driven by higher insurance service results.

item.title: Zurich posts stronger Q1’26 P&C growth as premiums rise 8%
item.description: Zurich Insurance Group reported continued momentum in its P&C business during the opening quarter of 2026, with insurance revenue rising 5% to $12 billion and gross written premiums increasing to $15.6 billion. Turning to Zurich’s Life business, the firm reported weaker like-for-like growth across parts of the segment, as declines in Europe, North America and Latin America offset growth in Asia Pacific. Latin America recorded the strongest regional growth in Q1 2026, with insurance revenue up 18% like-for-like to $946 million, compared with $770 million a year earlier.

item.title: WBN Survey: Global Business Leaders Shift Risk Strategy With Insurance
item.description: Global organization are moving from risk mitigation to a risk-led strategy as business leaders shift their approach to risk, influenced by geopolitical instability, economic uncertainty, and technological disruption. “Having the right risk strategy can make the difference between reacting to disruption and confidently shaping the future.” Forty-five percent of the 1,750 C-suite leaders surveyed listed risk management as the second most critical driver of corporate resilience. According to Worldwide Broker Network’s (WBN) “ New Risk Reality Report ,” resilience has become a competitive advantage, said senior business executives surveyed across the U.S., United Kingdom, Canada, France, Germany, and Spain.

item.title: Supply-Chain Stress That Peaked in COVID Heads Higher Again
item.description: The impact of the energy crisis on the global economy in recent months is showing up in gauges of supply-chain stress that flashed red during the pandemic, adding to reasons for central banks to be on guard for a recurrence of high inflation. The World Bank’s Global Supply Chain Stress Index is flirting with its pandemic peak. Fresh strains are reflected in the Federal Reserve Bank of New York’s Global Supply Chain Pressure Index.

item.title: Typhoon Season in Northwest Pacific Seen Most Active in a Decade
item.description: Typhoon activity in the Northwest Pacific Ocean is expected to be the highest this year since 2015, according to commercial forecaster Tropical Storm Risk. The European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts is also predicting an above-normal number of tropical storms in the Northwest Pacific this summer. The outlook comes after a below-average 2025 season, with typhoon activity roughly 35% below the historical average, according to Tropical Storm Risk .

item.title: Fisent Launches BizAI Workflow Automation Studio
item.description: Fisent Technologies (Toronto) has launched Fisent BizAI Studio, a self-service platform designed to enable enterprise users to configure, test and manage generative artificial intelligence-driven workflow automation processes. “Fisent BizAI Studio manifests three years of product development and ongoing capability enhancements into an intuitive, user-facing application,” comments Adrian Murray, Founder and CEO, Fisent. ITC Briefing: Fisent Targets Automation Gaps with AI That Understands Content AEGIS London Selects Fisent’s BizAI Solution to Optimize Key Business Processes

item.title: Simplifai Updates P&C Document Intelligence Platform
item.description: Simplifai (Oslo, Norway) has updated its artificial intelligence platform for property/casualty claims operations with new document intelligence capabilities intended to improve classification, extraction and review of insurance documents. “A claims operation can now stand up a new document type in minutes.” Simplifai says the platform is part of its Agentic Insurance System architecture, under which artificial intelligence agents are designed to support automation, workflow augmentation and end-to-end execution within claims operations. Simplifai says the platform combines large language models with insurance-specific document engineering capabilities, including handwriting recognition, image interpretation, form parsing and contextual analysis across multiple documents within a claim file.

item.title: HIVE Launches Aviation Reinsurance Division
item.description: (Image source: HIVE Underwriters homepage.) HIVE Underwriters (London) has launched an aviation reinsurance division as part of the company’s multi-class expansion strategy. HIVE has appointed Scott Bradbury and Joshua Down to lead the aviation reinsurance operation following completion of their obligations with their current employer. Bradbury joins HIVE after serving in aviation reinsurance underwriting leadership roles involving portfolio management across the global market.

item.title: The Big Dog Is Off the Tech Porch: State Farm as ‘Next Gen Good Neighbor’
item.description: You’d have to roll back the calendar a few decades to find a time when competitive moves by the nation’s biggest auto and home insurer were described with the phrase, “The big dog is off the porch.” Still, against the backdrop of competition surging in the personal lines insurance market, it was hard for this “mature” journalist not to draw a parallel to the price wars of the late 1990s and AI wars that seem to be revving up in 2026 when reading today’s State Farm media statement detailing the carrier’s “Next Gen Good Neighbor” vision. The statement, as well as blog items posted on State Farm’s website last week from President and Chief Executive Officer Jon Farney and from Joe Park, executive vice president and chief digital information officer , promise a future-ready carrier that will focus “on even faster and simpler claims service; more competitive prices that match price to risk; and data-driven underwriting.” Related State Farm content : A Next Gen Good Neighbor ; Empowering Next Gen Good Neighbors “Technology underpins Next Gen Good Neighbor, building on the personal relationships that remain the foundation of State Farm,” the statement says, revealing AI-powered tools, like one for State Farm agents that summarizes customers’ top-of-mind household concerns, and another that connects customers to support services at the start of a claims process. During an interview with Carrier Management , Farney didn’t buy into the idea that the timing of the announcement was meant to signal State Farm throwing down a tech gauntlet to competitors.

item.title: Once-in-a-lifetime opportunity: Asia’s data centre boom tests insurers
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Arity partners with Experian
item.description: Mobility data and analytics company Arity announced a partnership that connects its insurance driving intelligence with Experian’s insurance marketplace, giving auto insurance carriers a “faster, more scalable path” to incorporate driving behavior into quoting and qualification workflows. By connecting consumer education with FCRA‑compliant driving intelligence at the point of shopping, we’re giving carriers something they haven’t had before — scalable, carrier‑agnostic behavioral insight without the buildout.” – Gary Hallgren, President at Arity. By educating consumers first and grounding the experience in transparency and consent, we’re helping carriers access driving intelligence in a way that’s scalable, trusted, and built on consumer choice.” – Jeff Sadler, Senior Director of Insurance Operations at Experian Consumer Services.

item.title: Florida Insurer Safepoint Reveals 97% Revenue Surge in U.S. IPO Filing
item.description: It could cause a mini slowdown around the week of the SpaceX listing, if companies don’t want to compete for investor attention.” Founded in 2013, Tampa, Florida-based Safepoint is a property and casualty insurer focused on delivering insurance in coastal markets such as Florida and Louisiana, as well as other U.S. initial public offering, disclosing a 96.9% revenue jump last year as a strong run of new listings from Florida-based insurers extends into 2026. Safepoint on Friday filed its paperwork for a U.S.

item.title: 10 Year Study Finds No Real Benefit to Common Knee Surgery
item.description: In the 10-year clinical trial, researchers found that trimming a damaged meniscus — a procedure long believed to relieve pain—offers no real benefit over placebo surgery. A widely performed knee procedure known as partial meniscectomy may not deliver the benefits many patients expect, according to a new study out of the University of Helsinki. The surgery has long been based on the idea that knee pain, especially on the inner side, is caused by a meniscus tear that can be fixed surgically.

item.title: Farmers Insurance in California: HO Rates Up 1.5%; Bundlers Get 22% Discount
item.description: Farmers Insurance announced yesterday that the California Department of Insurance has approved the insurer’s introduction of a new homeowners insurance rating plan, including elements from Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara’s Sustainable Insurance Strategy. According to Farmers, the new homeowners insurance rating plan will adjust rates upwards by a statewide average of 1.5%, but new or existing customers who choose to bundle will generally see their rates drop, in general. “We continue to see encouraging signs that the California insurance marketplace is strengthening and we want to be well-positioned to grow and provide improved coverage offerings to California consumers,” said Behram Dinshaw, president of personal lines for Farmers Insurance, in a media statement.

item.title: Sompo continues Australia team expansion with addition of Everest’s Sophie Morrison
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Antares appoints Sri Ram Pappu as deputy Singapore CEO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Alt capital sustainability hinges on modeling, transparency, careful product sequencing: GC’s Rousseau
item.description: As alternative capital continues to integrate further into the global reinsurance market, in order for this to be truly sustainable the sector must prioritise better modeling, greater transparency and careful sequencing of product development so that capital can be deployed where it strengthens resilience, according to Laurent Rousseau of Guy Carpenter. “That convergence is not merely about adding supply; it is reshaping the industry’s economics and identity as reinsurers become originators, structurers, and distributors of risk.” He continued: “If this redesign is to be sustainable, the sector must prioritize better modeling, greater transparency around who ultimately bears losses, and careful sequencing of product development so that capital can be deployed where it truly strengthens resilience rather than amplifies systemic fragility.” Meanwhile, Guy Carpenter’s report also highlights how public catastrophe bond issuance continues to reach record levels as more players continue to utilise the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market. Rousseau’s comments come as Guy Carpenter has recently published a new report that examines the accelerating integration of alternative capital into the global reinsurance market and the implications for capacity, pricing, and market structure.

item.title: American Coastal lifting top of reinsurance tower to $1.6bn, adds more aggregate cover: CEO
item.description: The mid-year 2026 reinsurance renewal for American Coastal Insurance Company (AmCoastal) is “effectively complete” its CEO Brad Martz has explained, with the top of the tower lifting to over $1.6bn, while the company has added more aggregate protection as well. A year ago, American Coastal Insurance (AmCoastal) renewed its core catastrophe reinsurance program with first event limit of $1.33 billion at the June 2025 renewal . Martz explained, “Third, we have moved our lower layers to an all-perils basis that will allow us to non-renew the January 1st all other perils catastrophe reinsurance program next year, while maintaining robust protection against potential non-hurricane cat events.” Adding, “Lastly, we have more aggregate protection against frequency and severity resulting from a potentially active hurricane season.”

item.title: IAG eyes GWP of over $25bn by 2030 in new growth plan
item.description: At its Investor Day in Sydney, IAG has unveiled a refreshed strategy and new long-term financial targets under its Ambition 2030 plan, which aims to grow the group’s customer base to more than 11 million and lift gross written premium (GWP) above AU$25 billion. Over the past five years, we have delivered on our targets and laid solid foundations for our new Ambition 2030 goals. Having successfully delivered on targets set in 2021, IAG said that it is well-positioned to deliver Ambition 2030 and four strategic priorities: Customer Obsession; Insurance Excellence; Future Fit Operations; and Exceptional People.
